findings.title: Host does not look like a Raspberry Pi
findings.summary: pi-doctor detected a Linux-like system identity, but it could not confirm Raspberry Pi hardware.
suggested_actions: Why this matters: later diagnostics rely on Raspberry Pi-specific firmware and device metadata.
What to run next: confirm `/proc/device-tree/model` exists on the target Raspberry Pi and rerun `pi-doctor check` there.

findings.title: vcgencmd is not available
findings.summary: pi-doctor could not read Raspberry Pi firmware throttling telemetry because `vcgencmd` is missing.
suggested_actions: Why this matters: undervoltage and firmware throttling signals come from Raspberry Pi firmware telemetry.
What to run next: verify you are on Raspberry Pi OS or install the Raspberry Pi firmware utilities before rerunning `pi-doctor explain throttling`.
